english_name: The Epistle of Paul the Apostle to the Galatians

verses.latin_vulgate: Deinde post annos quatuordecim, iterum ascendi Jerosolymam cum Barnaba, assumpto et Tito.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἕπειτα διὰ δεκατεσσάρων ἐτῶν πάλιν ἀνέβην εἰς ἱεροσόλυμα μετὰ βαρναβᾶ συμπαραλαβὼν καὶ τίτον
verses.king_james: Then fourteen years after I went up again to Jerusalem with Barnabas, and took Titus with *me* also.

verses.latin_vulgate: Ascendi autem secundum revelationem: et contuli cum illis Evangelium, quod prædico in gentibus, seorsum autem iis qui videbantur aliquid esse: ne forte in vacuum currerem, aut cucurrissem.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἀνέβην δὲ κατὰ ἀποκάλυψιν καὶ ἀνεθέμην αὐτοῖς τὸ εὐαγγέλιον ὃ κηρύσσω ἐν τοῖς ἔθνεσιν κατ᾽ ἰδίαν δὲ τοῖς δοκοῦσιν μήπως εἰς κενὸν τρέχω ἢ ἔδραμον
verses.king_james: And I went up by revelation, and communicated unto them that gospel which I preach among the Gentiles, but privately to them which were of reputation, lest by any means I should run, or had run, in vain.

verses.latin_vulgate: Sed neque Titus, qui mecum erat, cum esset gentilis, compulsus est circumcidi:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἀλλ᾽ οὐδὲ τίτος ὁ σὺν ἐμοί ἕλλην ὤν ἠναγκάσθη περιτμηθῆναι
verses.king_james: But neither Titus, who was with me, being a Greek, was compelled to be circumcised:

verses.latin_vulgate: sed propter subintroductos falsos fratres, qui subintroierunt explorare libertatem nostram, quam habemus in Christo Jesu, ut nos in servitutem redigerent.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: διὰ δὲ τοὺς παρεισάκτους ψευδαδέλφους οἵτινες παρεισῆλθον κατασκοπῆσαι τὴν ἐλευθερίαν ἡμῶν ἣν ἔχομεν ἐν χριστῷ ἰησοῦ ἵνα ἡμᾶς καταδουλώσωνται
verses.king_james: And that because of false brethren unawares brought in, who came in privily to spy out our liberty which we have in Christ Jesus, that they might bring us into bondage:

verses.latin_vulgate: Quibus neque ad horam cessimus subjectione, ut veritas Evangelii permaneat apud vos:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: οἷς οὐδὲ πρὸς ὥραν εἴξαμεν τῇ ὑποταγῇ ἵνα ἡ ἀλήθεια τοῦ εὐαγγελίου διαμείνῃ πρὸς ὑμᾶς
verses.king_james: To whom we gave place by subjection, no, not for an hour; that the truth of the gospel might continue with you.

verses.latin_vulgate: ab iis autem, qui videbantur esse aliquid (quales aliquando fuerint, nihil mea interest: Deus personam hominis non accipit): mihi enim qui videbantur esse aliquid, nihil contulerunt.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἀπὸ δὲ τῶν δοκούντων εἶναί τι ὁποῖοί ποτε ἦσαν οὐδέν μοι διαφέρει πρόσωπον θεὸς ἀνθρώπου οὐ λαμβάνει ἐμοὶ γὰρ οἱ δοκοῦντες οὐδὲν προσανέθεντο
verses.king_james: But of these who seemed to be somewhat, (whatsoever they were, it maketh no matter to me: God accepteth no man's person:) for they who seemed *to be somewhat* in conference added nothing to me:

verses.latin_vulgate: Sed e contra cum vidissent quod creditum est mihi Evangelium præputii, sicut et Petro circumcisionis
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἀλλὰ τοὐναντίον ἰδόντες ὅτι πεπίστευμαι τὸ εὐαγγέλιον τῆς ἀκροβυστίας καθὼς πέτρος τῆς περιτομῆς
verses.king_james: But contrariwise, when they saw that the gospel of the uncircumcision was committed unto me, as *the gospel* of the circumcision *was* unto Peter;

verses.latin_vulgate: (qui enim operatus est Petro in apostolatum circumcisionis, operatus est et mihi inter gentes):
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὁ γὰρ ἐνεργήσας πέτρῳ εἰς ἀποστολὴν τῆς περιτομῆς ἐνήργησεν καὶ ἐμοὶ εἰς τὰ ἔθνη
verses.king_james: (For he that wrought effectually in Peter to the apostleship of the circumcision, the same was mighty in me toward the Gentiles:)

verses.latin_vulgate: et cum cognovissent gratiam, quæ data est mihi, Jacobus, et Cephas, et Joannes, qui videbantur columnæ esse, dextras dederunt mihi, et Barnabæ societatis: ut nos in gentes, ipsi autem in circumcisionem:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ γνόντες τὴν χάριν τὴν δοθεῖσάν μοι ἰάκωβος καὶ κηφᾶς καὶ ἰωάννης οἱ δοκοῦντες στῦλοι εἶναι δεξιὰς ἔδωκαν ἐμοὶ καὶ βαρναβᾷ κοινωνίας ἵνα ἡμεῖς εἰς τὰ ἔθνη αὐτοὶ δὲ εἰς τὴν περιτομήν
verses.king_james: And when James, Cephas, and John, who seemed to be pillars, perceived the grace that was given unto me, they gave to me and Barnabas the right hands of fellowship; that we *should go* unto the heathen, and they unto the circumcision.

verses.latin_vulgate: tantum ut pauperum memores essemus, quod etiam sollicitus fui hoc ipsum facere.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: μόνον τῶν πτωχῶν ἵνα μνημονεύωμεν ὃ καὶ ἐσπούδασα αὐτὸ τοῦτο ποιῆσαι
verses.king_james: Only *they would* that we should remember the poor; the same which I also was forward to do.

verses.latin_vulgate: Cum autem venisset Cephas Antiochiam, in faciem ei restiti, quia reprehensibilis erat.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὅτε δὲ ἦλθεν πέτρος εἰς ἀντιόχειαν κατὰ πρόσωπον αὐτῷ ἀντέστην ὅτι κατεγνωσμένος ἦν
verses.king_james: But when Peter was come to Antioch, I withstood him to the face, because he was to be blamed.

verses.latin_vulgate: Prius enim quam venirent quidam a Jacobo, cum gentibus edebat: cum autem venissent, subtrahebat, et segregabat se, timens eos qui ex circumcisione erant.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: πρὸ τοῦ γὰρ ἐλθεῖν τινας ἀπὸ ἰακώβου μετὰ τῶν ἐθνῶν συνήσθιεν ὅτε δὲ ἦλθον ὑπέστελλεν καὶ ἀφώριζεν ἑαυτόν φοβούμενος τοὺς ἐκ περιτομῆς
verses.king_james: For before that certain came from James, he did eat with the Gentiles: but when they were come, he withdrew and separated himself, fearing them which were of the circumcision.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et simulationi ejus consenserunt ceteri Judæi, ita ut et Barnabas duceretur ab eis in illam simulationem.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ συνυπεκρίθησαν αὐτῷ καὶ οἱ λοιποὶ ἰουδαῖοι ὥστε καὶ βαρναβᾶς συναπήχθη αὐτῶν τῇ ὑποκρίσει
verses.king_james: And the other Jews dissembled likewise with him; insomuch that Barnabas also was carried away with their dissimulation.

verses.latin_vulgate: Sed cum vidissem quod non recte ambularent ad veritatem Evangelii, dixi Cephæ coram omnibus: Si tu, cum Judæus sis, gentiliter vivis, et non judaice: quomodo gentes cogis judaizare?
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἀλλ᾽ ὅτε εἶδον ὅτι οὐκ ὀρθοποδοῦσιν πρὸς τὴν ἀλήθειαν τοῦ εὐαγγελίου εἶπον τῷ πέτρῳ ἔμπροσθεν πάντων Εἰ σὺ ἰουδαῖος ὑπάρχων ἐθνικῶς ζῇς καὶ οὐκ ἰουδαϊκῶς τί τὰ ἔθνη ἀναγκάζεις ἰουδαΐζειν
verses.king_james: But when I saw that they walked not uprightly according to the truth of the gospel, I said unto Peter before *them* all, If thou, being a Jew, livest after the manner of Gentiles, and not as do the Jews, why compellest thou the Gentiles to live as do the Jews?

verses.latin_vulgate: Nos natura Judæi, et non ex gentibus peccatores.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἡμεῖς φύσει ἰουδαῖοι καὶ οὐκ ἐξ ἐθνῶν ἁμαρτωλοί
verses.king_james: We *who are* Jews by nature, and not sinners of the Gentiles,

verses.latin_vulgate: Scientes autem quod non justificatur homo ex operibus legis, nisi per fidem Jesu Christi: et nos in Christo Jesu credimus, ut justificemur ex fide Christi, et non ex operibus legis: propter quod ex operibus legis non justificabitur omnis caro.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: εἰδότες ὅτι οὐ δικαιοῦται ἄνθρωπος ἐξ ἔργων νόμου ἐὰν μὴ διὰ πίστεως ἰησοῦ χριστοῦ καὶ ἡμεῖς εἰς χριστὸν ἰησοῦν ἐπιστεύσαμεν ἵνα δικαιωθῶμεν ἐκ πίστεως χριστοῦ καὶ οὐκ ἐξ ἔργων νόμου διότι οὐ δικαιωθήσεται ἐξ ἔργων νόμου πᾶσα σάρξ
verses.king_james: Knowing that a man is not justified by the works of the law, but by the faith of Jesus Christ, even we have believed in Jesus Christ, that we might be justified by the faith of Christ, and not by the works of the law: for by the works of the law shall no flesh be justified.

verses.latin_vulgate: Quod si quærentes justificari in Christo, inventi sumus et ipsi peccatores, numquid Christus peccati minister est? Absit.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: εἰ δὲ ζητοῦντες δικαιωθῆναι ἐν χριστῷ εὑρέθημεν καὶ αὐτοὶ ἁμαρτωλοί ἆρα χριστὸς ἁμαρτίας διάκονος μὴ γένοιτο
verses.king_james: But if, while we seek to be justified by Christ, we ourselves also are found sinners, *is* therefore Christ the minister of sin? God forbid.

verses.latin_vulgate: Si enim quæ destruxi, iterum hæc ædifico: prævaricatorem me constituo.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: εἰ γὰρ ἃ κατέλυσα ταῦτα πάλιν οἰκοδομῶ παραβάτην ἐμαυτὸν συνίστημι
verses.king_james: For if I build again the things which I destroyed, I make myself a transgressor.

verses.latin_vulgate: Ego enim per legem, legi mortuus sum, ut Deo vivam: Christo confixus sum cruci.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἐγὼ γὰρ διὰ νόμου νόμῳ ἀπέθανον ἵνα θεῷ ζήσω
verses.king_james: For I through the law am dead to the law, that I might live unto God.

verses.latin_vulgate: Vivo autem, jam non ego: vivit vero in me Christus. Quod autem nunc vivo in carne: in fide vivo Filii Dei, qui dilexit me, et tradidit semetipsum pro me.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: χριστῷ συνεσταύρωμαι ζῶ δὲ οὐκέτι ἐγώ ζῇ δὲ ἐν ἐμοὶ χριστός ὃ δὲ νῦν ζῶ ἐν σαρκί ἐν πίστει ζῶ τῇ τοῦ υἱοῦ τοῦ θεοῦ τοῦ ἀγαπήσαντός με καὶ παραδόντος ἑαυτὸν ὑπὲρ ἐμοῦ
verses.king_james: I am crucified with Christ: nevertheless I live; yet not I, but Christ liveth in me: and the life which I now live in the flesh I live by the faith of the Son of God, who loved me, and gave himself for me.

verses.latin_vulgate: Non abjicio gratiam Dei. Si enim per legem justitia, ergo gratis Christus mortuus est.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: οὐκ ἀθετῶ τὴν χάριν τοῦ θεοῦ εἰ γὰρ διὰ νόμου δικαιοσύνη ἄρα χριστὸς δωρεὰν ἀπέθανεν
verses.king_james: I do not frustrate the grace of God: for if righteousness *come* by the law, then Christ is dead in vain.
